text: The study investigated the molecular mechanism of two Tartary buckwheat genotypes with contrasting sensitivity to LN. LN improved primary and lateral root growth of the LN-sensitive genotype. The LN-insensitive genotype showed no response.

Autotetraploid sorghum inbreds have higher kernel weight, seed yield, and protein and amino acid content than diploid sorghum inbreds. Stalk height and panicle length of autotetraploids are not different from diploids. Seed set of autotetraploids is lower in early generations.

Sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) is often not damaged by southern root-knot nematodes (RKN). Some sorghum genotypes support greater RKN population densities than others. These higher nematode populations increase damage risk to subsequently planted susceptible crops.

Illumina sequencing was applied to investigate drought-responsive miRNAs from S. italica inbred An04-4783 seedlings under control and drought conditions. Degradome sequencing confirmed the targets of these miRNAs.
Composite interval mapping identified six QTLs for 100-seed weight and five QTLs for days to flowering. Three genomic regions harbored QTLs for both traits.

text: 375 mung bean genotypes from the Iowa (IA) diversity panel were studied for root traits. Genome-wide association studies of root-related traits were conducted using Automated Root Image Analysis (ARIA). 9,000 digital images were collected at three-time points: days 12, 15, and 18 after germination. Broad sense heritability for days 15 (0.22-0.73) and 18 (0.23-0.87) was higher than for day 12 (0.24-0.51).

QTL analysis of nitrogen use efficiency (NUE) in barley was conducted using AFLP markers and 94 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) from the Prisma x Apex mapping population. In total, 41 QTLs were detected on 6 chromosomes for 18 traits.

text: Three QTLs for barley GOA were mapped on chromosomes 2H and 7H. The major QTL QGOA-2H-2 was mapped on chromosome 2H, flanked by markers KDH and GBM1498, and explained 63.92% of phenotypic variation.

A winter wheat-summer foxtail millet-winter wheat rotation is typical, so early maturity of foxtail millet is critical. The objective was to develop a summer foxtail millet cultivar with early maturity and promising agronomical performance. 'Canggu 5' (Reg.

In phospholipid metabolism, phospholipase D genes were down-regulated and inositol and ethanolamine contents increased in Zhonggu2. Glycine and serine relative contents increased, and the serine hydroxymethyltransferase gene in glycine-serine metabolism was upregulated.

Common buckwheat (Fagopyrum esculentum) is a staple food crop in southwest China. Drought stress is a major limiting factor that decreases crop production. To reveal the molecular mechanism of common buckwheat in response to drought stress, we performed a transcriptomics study to evaluate gene expression profiles during PEG-mediated drought treatment.

Association mapping was done to understand drought and heat tolerance genetic basis in chickpea. Phenotypic data for drought tolerance root traits, heat tolerance, yield and yield component traits were generated on a reference set (300 accessions, including 211 mini-core collection) from multiple seasons and locations in India and Africa.

Si treatment altered key metabolic pathways in oat leaves, including tryptophan metabolism, glyoxylate and dicarboxylate metabolism, porphyrin metabolism, and chlorophyll metabolism. This provides insights into Si-mediated enhancement of oat resistance to stem rust.

Using a linkage map with 367 markers (DArT and SSRs) and an in vitro assay for germination stimulant activity towards Striga asiatica in 354 recombinant inbred lines from SRN39 (low stimulant) x Shanqui Red (high stimulant), the lgs gene was mapped on SBI-05 between microsatellite markers SB3344 and SB3352 at distances of 0.5 and 1.5 cM, respectively. The fine-mapped lgs region was delimited to a 5.8 cM interval with markers SB3344, SB3346 and SB3343 at 0.5, 0.7 and 0.9 cM, respectively.

101 oat genotypes were evaluated. The genotypes comprised 51 cultivars and 50 breeding lines from eight Canadian oat breeding programs. They were evaluated for seedling reactions to six common Pca races and to a bulk Pca inoculum in greenhouse trials. They were also evaluated for adult plant resistance (APR) to natural Pca populations in field trials in 2014 and 2015. 66 genotypes showed resistance to at least one race. 22 of these were resistant to all six races.

Five ontologies (Gene Ontology, Trait Ontology, Plant Ontology, Growth Ontology, Environment Ontology) integrated drought information. Target genes linked to QTLs controlling yield and stress tolerance in sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) and related species were identified.
Under drought stress, the auxin, cytokinin, and brassinosteroid signaling pathways were suppressed in Longyan 3. The abscisic acid and jasmonic acid signaling pathways were activated in DA92-2F6.

Drought stress exacerbated cell membrane damage, reduced chlorophyll content, and impaired antioxidant enzyme activity in both buckwheat varieties DK19 and K33. DK19 exhibited greater drought resistance than K33.

Hull color (HC) is a major indicator of nutritional quality in foxtail millet. To identify the gene or QTL for HC, RAD-seq analysis was used to reveal genome-wide SNPs and genotype the F-2 progeny from a cross between cultivar Heizhigu with green HC and cultivar Changnong 35 with yellow HC.

Genome-wide association mapping identified 39 significant marker-trait associations (MTAs) for pods/plant, seeds/pod, seed weight, and yield/plant. These include 15 major, 15 stable, and 13 both major and stable MTAs. Among the 39 MTAs, 29 are new, while 10 validate earlier studies. Seven markers are associated with multiple traits.

Pc45 was studied in the crosses 'AC Morgan'/Pc45 and 'Kasztan'/Pc45. F-2 progenies and F-2:3 families of both populations were inoculated with crown rust isolate CR258 (race NTGG). Single gene segregation ratios were observed.

Drought stress affects quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa Willd.) production. The drought-tolerant quinoa genotype Dianli 129 was screened. Its seedling leaves were studied after drought and rewatering treatments using transcriptomics and targeted metabolomics.

Sorghum exhibits enhanced water uptake and reduced dissipation in arid environments to withstand drought stress. Sorghum exhibits drought resistance by adjusting osmotic potential, scavenging reactive oxygen species, and changing antioxidant enzyme activities.
Molecular markers and genomics are essential in sorghum breeding. Next-generation sequencing enables genome-wide InDel identification and efficient construction of user-friendly PCR InDel markers.

The panicle length and weight decreased, and the empty abortive rate increased, resulting in yield reductions of 14.9% and 36.9%. Photosynthesis in millet significantly decreased, including net photosynthesis rate, stomatal conductance, and transpiration rate, especially under HD treatment with slow recovery after rehydration.

Path-coefficient analysis revealed that grains number on branches was the most dominant component of seed-setting rate, followed by grains number on stem. Grains number on branches had the greatest positive direct effect on seed-setting rate.

A high-density genetic map was constructed using a recombinant inbred line population between sorghum Tx623A and sudangrass Sa with RAD-seq. QTL mapping was performed for plant height (PH), stem diameter (SD), tiller number (TN), total shoot fresh weight (FW) and total shoot dry weight (DW). The map has 1,065 markers and a total length of 1191.7 cM.

The gene expression of SiHKTs under salt stress showed significant differences in different tissues. At the early stage of salt stress, SiHKTs transfer K+ into aboveground. Under salt stress, SiHKTs prevent sodium toxicity in root.

A novel wheat-rye derivative line LCR4 was developed through hybridization between hexaploid triticale line Currency and common wheat cultivar Jimai 22 (JM22). LCR4 was confirmed as a T2DS2DL-2RL translocation line via genomic in situ hybridization, multi-color fluorescence in situ hybridization, molecular marker analysis, and wheat SNP-arrays genotyping.

All SbCIPKs contain a conserved SNF1-like kinase domain in the N terminus and a NAF motif in the C terminus. Stress- and light-responsive cis-elements are present in the promoter regions of sorghum CIPKs.

Significant QTLs for SDW were mapped on LG10 with 15.5% PVE under well-watered conditions. A putative QTL for SDW was mapped on LG10 with 10.10% PVE under drought-stressed conditions in the F-3 population. Significant QTLs for NS, NDP, SW, and PW were mapped on LG4 (nearest markers: 4181663 and 4175954) with overlapping intervals and explained 21.9%, 21.8%, 23.5%, and 19.9% PVE respectively under well-watered conditions in the F-4 population. These are major QTLs controlling these traits.
Salicylic acid treatment reduced membrane permeability and Na+ content under salt stress. Yajin 13 had lower Na+ content and greater salinity tolerance than Yajin 71.
Ten breeder's MQTLs were selected from 25 validated MQTLs. Synteny analysis revealed orthologous relationships between eight breeder's MQTLs and 45 wheat MQTLs.

The highest predictive ability for DM was 0.34 using 137,191 SNPs. For HD it was 0.77 using 185,297 SNPs. For RUST it was 0.55 using 188,832 SNPs. Genomic prediction can optimize tetraploid ryegrass breeding.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is a cereal crop. Its yield is limited by cold stress. We identified a novel gene, SiCST1 (Cold Stress Tolerance in Setaria italica 1), in foxtail millet through RNA-Seq of cold-stressed seedlings.

text: Transactivation analysis showed that SbSNAC1's transactivation activity is in the C-terminal region, with no activity in the conserved NAC-domain at the N-terminus. Subcellular localization assays using SbSNAC1 fragments fused with green fluorescent protein revealed that SbSNAC1 localizes in the nucleus, with the nuclear localization signal in the N-terminal section.
Tartary buckwheat is an abiotic resistant coarse cereal, but its stress tolerance mechanisms are largely unknown. MYB transcription factors regulate plant stress tolerance.

A high-density genetic linkage map was developed from a population of 172 F-2:12 recombinant inbred lines (RILs). The RILs were derived from a cross between the barley variety Yongjiabaidamai (YJ) and Hua 30 (H30). The map was used to map QTLs for spike length (SL), rachis node number (SRN), and spike density (SD). A total of 50 additive QTLs (LOD > 3) were mapped in four environments. Four of these QTLs were stable and major.
GWAS identified 24 SNPs significantly associated with traits. They include three SNPs with HT, four with TN, four with PL, three with Brix, and ten with RS.
BC3F3 families had genetic variation for selecting malting quality and agronomic traits. Ten breeding lines with improved beta-amylase thermostability also had improved yields and grain plumpness.

Different oat cultivars and treatment periods showed significant differences in gene expression after drought and salt stress treatments. Genes AsTCP021, AsTCP033, AsTCP044, AsTCP053, and AsTCP058 showed significant expression changes under both treatments. AsTCP053 was significantly upregulated at 24 h of stress treatment and showed a more sensitive response to salt stress.
The rice Erect Panicle2 (EP2) ortholog is a candidate gene in Pm 2-2. EP2 regulates panicle erectness and panicle length in rice.

F. esculentum and F. tataricum differ in water stress resistance. Water stress decreased leaf production, leaf fresh weight, leaf dry weight, stomatal conductance, transpiration rate, and photosynthesis rate in F. esculentum, but not in F. tataricum.
FtMYB10 from Tartary buckwheat was induced by ABA and drought. Transgenic lines were less sensitive to ABA in a germination test.

215 candidate genes were identified at these loci, including transcription factors, protein kinases, and other drought-responsive protein coding genes. Superior alleles associated with drought tolerance were identified and positively selected during breeding.

DEGs and DEMs participate in starch and sucrose metabolism and flavonoid biosynthesis. These processes improve drought tolerance in quinoa. Drought tolerance in quinoa correlates with gene expression differences, metabolite accumulation, and recovery after rewatering.

Reduction in N, P, K, Ca and Mg was found in all cultivars/lines, while Fe remained unchanged. Cultivars/lines NM-2006 and 8005 are drought tolerant. Cultivars/lines 97,006 and 97,001 are drought sensitive.

Integrated metabolomic and transcriptomic analyses compared two cultivars, elite variety Jingu 21 (JG21) and traditional landrace Niumaobai (NMB), during two grain-filling stages. 552 metabolites were identified, with 144 differentially abundant.

An F-6 RIL population from the cross Yugu 5 x Jigu 31 was phenotyped for leaf blast reactions in six field trials. An ultra-density genetic linkage map was constructed using 35,065 SNP markers from sequencing the RIL population.

A cross between Aininghuang and Jingu 21 produced an F-2 population of 543 foxtail millet plants. A high-density linkage map was constructed using RAD-seq. QTLs for 11 agronomic traits (PH, PL, PD, PNL, FID, SID, PW, GW, TGW, PT, PC) were mapped based on this map.

Resistance loci against pathotypes from Texas and Puerto Rico are flanked by SSR markers Ch5-55.0 and Ch5-56.1. These genes regulate flowering time, pollen germination, and growth.

27 loci on six barley chromosomes had resistance effects to at least one of five isolates. Almost all previously reported chromosomal regions for powdery mildew resistance genes were detected, plus a possible additional locus on chromosome 7.

A recombinant inbred line (RIL) population of 305 lines was derived from JG21 (high-carotenoid) and JG25 (low-carotenoid). The RIL population showed continuous phenotypic variation and significant transgressive segregation for carotenoid components (lutein, zeaxanthin, beta-cryptoxanthin) and kernel color, with zeaxanthin surpassing the higher parent.

The genes encoding glutathione S-transferase (GST), peroxidase (POD), phosphate transporter (PT), glucan endonuclease (GLU), beta-galactosidase (BGAL), cellulose synthase (CES), trichome birefringence-like protein (TBL), glycine-rich cell wall structural protein (GRP), glucosyltransferase (GT), GDSL esterase/lipase (GELP), cytochrome P450 (CYP), and jasmonate-induced protein (JIP) were differentially expressed. These genes may mediate osmotic adjustment, cell wall organization, reactive oxygen species scavenging, and plant hormone signaling to regulate responses to ethylene and salt stress.

A field study tested the integrated effects of two planting densities (67,500 and 90,000 plants ha⁻¹) and three N rates (0, 180, and 360 kg ha⁻¹) on maize production. The higher planting density increased grain yield (GY), N use efficiency (NUE), agronomic N efficiency (AEN), and N partial productivity (PFP_N). It also decreased N₂O emission and greenhouse gas intensity.
29 putative HAK/KUP/KT proteins were identified from the Setaria italica genome database. These genes were distributed in seven chromosomes of foxtail millet.
Two millet cultivars, drought-resistant Yugu1 (YG) and drought-sensitive An04 (An), were used. Water use efficiency increased by 18.4% in An and 63.2% in YG under drought stress.

Except for the QTLs for plant height and NUE based on grain yield, no QTLs detected for a trait in 2005 expressed themselves in 2008 across nitrogen levels. QTLs controlling some traits were co-located each year. QTLs for many traits were detected on the same chromosome near the denso locus.

Base editing targeted the SiALS and SiACC genes of foxtail millet using CBE and ABE. CBE targeting SiALS created a homozygous herbicide-tolerant mutant plant.

Carbon fixation in photosynthetic organisms and plant hormone signal transduction were enriched under drought stress. Expression levels of genes related to stress response and carbon metabolism were altered under drought stress.

Histochemical staining, interaction studies, and differential expression profiling suggest CaHSFA5 interacts with ROS scavenging genes to regulate ROS homeostasis for heat stress tolerance in chickpea. Heterologous expression and heat stress screening validated CaHSFA5's function in heat stress tolerance.

Lipidome and transcriptome analyses were applied to analyze the responses of membrane lipids to drought stress in two sorghum cultivars: drought-sensitive cv. Hongyingzi and drought-tolerant cv. Kangsi.
The morphological marker Trit is associated with a major QTL for resistance component traits. The QTL correspond to QTL or genes for insect resistance in syntenic maize genomic regions.
Gene-phenotype association analysis detected 113 genes associated with flowering time in barley. Two-way epistasis analysis revealed 19 pairs of gene-gene interactions controlling flowering time.

Transcriptome analysis identified 661 LN-responsive differentially expressed genes (DEGs). Among them, 17 genes are related to N transport and assimilation, and 29 genes are involved in hormone biosynthesis and signaling. These genes may play a role in Tartary buckwheat root development under LN.
Two QTLs were identified for biomass crystallinity index. This trait is inversely correlated with conversion efficiency in bioenergy grasses.

2792 transcription factors and 1223 transcriptional regulators were identified. 318 TFs and 149 TRs were differentially expressed under heat stress. 315 TFs and 128 TRs were differentially expressed under drought stress.

The oat cultivar Baiyan 2 at the three-leaf stage was used to investigate drought resistance. Seedlings were treated with polyethylene glycol (PEG) to simulate drought stress. Photosynthetic parameters and physicochemical indices were measured at 6 h and 12 h and compared to the control at 0 h.

DEGs involved in ROS detoxification in HN16 worked through different means. Analysis of three DEGs encoding APX and GPX revealed that SbAPx1, SbGPx1, and SbGPx2 all played an important role in RMES1-mediated resistance to aphids.

A high-density genetic map was constructed for the cross Tx623A x S722 using 103 recombinant inbred lines (RILs). The map contains 1610 markers and spans 2329 cM. QTL mapping identified 19 QTLs: two for plant height (PH), three for stem diameter (SD), four for tiller number (TN), four for fresh weight (FW), and five for dry weight (DW).
Foxtail millet (Setaria italica L.) resists drought stress. Its drought stress resistance molecular mechanisms are unclear.
13 of 15 alleles were polymorphic. The SSR marker had an average PIC of 0.205.

Based on a high-density variation map, we identified genome-level evidence for local adaptation. Siprr37 with transposon insertion contributes to the fitness of foxtail millet in the northeastern ecoregion.

Narrow leaf width is correlated with greater intrinsic water use efficiency in C-4 species. This study evaluated the correlation of leaf width with whole-plant TE and investigated the genetic control of leaf width in sorghum.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is threatened by rust disease caused by Uromyces setariae-italicae (Usi). The NAC transcription factor family is a plant-specific regulatory gene family involved in stress responses.

A novel QTL qRYM-7H/qTFRYM-7H for barley yellow mosaic resistance was identified by GWAS and linkage analysis. Candidate genes for qRYM-7H/qTFRYM-7H were predicted based on transcriptome sequencing and qPCR analysis.

The QTL on CHR 6H has the largest effect, explaining up to 20.9% of phenotypic variance. FLT interacts with flag leaf width and area, and correlates with fertile tiller number, spike row types, kernel number per spike, and heading date.

A chromosome-anchored genetic map was developed for an oat recombinant inbred population. SNP markers linked to QTL affecting root growth response to Al were identified. Three QTL on chromosomes 7C-17A, 13A, and 19A confer Al tolerance. They were identified using primary root regrowth of recombinant inbred lines from the cross between UFRGS 17 (Al tolerant) and UFRGS 930598-6 (Al sensitive).

Seedling resistance was evaluated as infection type (IT) using isolates of four pathogen races (DBD, SGD, TGN, and TJS). Seventeen QTL were detected, including ten at the adult plant stage, five at the seedling stage, and two shared across stages.

An integrated genomic strategy delineates a gene and alleles for a major QTL governing heat stress tolerance in chickpea. This can accelerate marker-assisted breeding and gene editing to develop high-yielding heat stress tolerant chickpea varieties.

GO and KEGG pathway analysis identified DEGs enriched in photosynthesis, chlorophyll metabolism, amino acid metabolism, and carbohydrate metabolism in three cultivars. 46 genes had transcription change trends consistent with drought resistance trends among three foxtail millet cultivars.

Four Sorghum bicolor genotypes were screened in a dry-down experiment. Different drought response strategies were identified among the four genotypes and the two Sorghum races. The durra race is more drought tolerant than the caudatum race. Within the durra race, genotype IS22330 is drought tolerant.

A GWAS used 87 barley landraces (39 hulled, 48 hulless) with 191,098 SNP markers. The population had four sub-populations (POP1 to POP4). Beta-glucan content in POP2 was significantly higher than in other groups. Most hulless barley landraces in POP2 are from the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau in China.

The study identified 54 bZIP proteins in V. radiata and 50 in V. angularis. It surveyed bZIP gene characteristics, including structure, chromosome distribution, and motif composition. Phylogenetic analysis divided VrbZIP and VabZIP proteins into ten clades.

SSR marker CEDG304 on LG9 of yardlong bean associated with resistance. High ammonium toxicity severely affects plant growth and crop production. Breeding high NH4+-tolerant and high-yield plants is urgent. The molecular mechanisms of plant response and tolerance to high NH4+ are poorly understood.
Seven CAMTA genes were identified in the quinoa genome. The expression patterns of CqCAMTAs were analyzed in root and leaf tissues.

Grain yield of Sorghum bicolor is influenced by variation in traits including tiller number, plant height, and time of anthesis. Sorghum is adaptable to drought and high temperature. Group 3 LEA genes from barley and rice play a crucial role in abiotic stress tolerance.

Transcriptome sequencing was performed on sorghum seedling leaves before and after cold treatment. Candidate genes for cold tolerance and regulation pathways for photosynthesis under cold stress were identified.

text: A total of 2155 and 3033 candidate genes involved in domestication, and 84 and 180 candidate genes related to improvement, were identified at the seedling and filling stages of broomcorn millet, respectively. Genes related to metabolites, stress resistance, and plant hormones were widely selected during domestication and improvement. Some genes were exclusively selected in either domestication or improvement stages. Higher selection pressure was detected in the domestication process.
12 SbPUB genes contain MYB-related elements, which are regulators of flavonoid biosynthesis. These results are consistent with a previous multi-omics analysis of sorghum under salt stress.

Genetic improvements in feedstock quality, including biomass total yield and conversion efficiency, are needed for lignocellulosic bioenergy. Studies have identified candidate genes and QTL associated with total biomass accumulation and grain production in maize and sorghum.

Chlorophyll fluorescence parameters were not affected by water stress in either species. Chlorophyll content increased in water-stressed plants in both species. Oxidative stress occurred in both species under water stress. Antioxidant content increased in F. tataricum.

Heterologous expression of SbCASP4 increased salt stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana. Under 50 mM NaCl treatment, SbCASP4-expression lines had less leaf Na+, lower PI accumulation in stele, smaller oxidative damage, higher salinity threshold, longer root length, and higher expression of Casparian strip formation genes compared to wild type and casp5 mutants.
Five QTL-regions were validated. They showed consistent effect in the GWAS population and the breeding material.

The phenotype of 126 RILs and their parents Tx623A (sorghum) and Sa (Sudangrass) was characterized for two years. The phenotypes included five forage quality traits: crude protein (CP), neutral detergent fiber (NDF), acid detergent fiber (ADF), acid detergent lignin (ADL), and hemicellulose (HC) content. 19 QTLs controlling these traits were mapped in the RIL population between sorghum Tx623A and sudangrass Sa.

High-yielding varieties had 12-13.7% higher stem base hardness and 6.3-11.5% higher middle stem hardness than medium- and low-yield varieties. They also showed increased dry matter accumulation, decreased effective branch number, increased main ear length, and increased 1000-grain weight.
SiLRR-RLK genes are unevenly distributed across chromosomes. Several SiLRR-RLK family members are involved in stress response pathways under biotic and abiotic stress conditions.
Candidate markers linked to PHS were located near these QTL regions. Markers linked to these QTLs were effective for selecting PHS-tolerant lines in other segregating populations.

Five stable QTLs are co-located on chromosome 3H flanked by markers bpb6282426 and bpb4786261. They are Qgna.sau-3H for grain N concentration, Qtna.sau-3H for total N accumulation per plant, Qnhi.sau-3H for N harvest index, Qnutegy.sau-3H for N utilization efficiency for grain yield, and Qanutedm.sau-3H.1 for N utilization efficiency for aboveground dry matter. These QTL clusters control NUE traits at seedling and maturity stages.

text: Metabolome test results showed that after aphid infestation, naringenin and genistein were significantly upregulated in the aphid-resistant variety HN16 and the aphid-susceptible variety BTX623. Luteolin was significantly upregulated only in BTX623. Naringenin, genistein, and luteolin play important roles in plant resistance to aphid infestation.
SSR markers were developed from genomic sequence comparison of Setaria genotypes. These SSR markers are highly polymorphic and effective.

Sixteen QTLs for grain length, grain width and thousand-grain weight were detected on chromosomes 1H, 2H, 4H, 5H, and 6H. A major QTL qGL1, with 16.7% phenotypic variance, was located on 1H. qGL1 is a new QTL affecting grain length.

The RI population was characterized under drought and non-drought conditions for traits of post-flowering drought tolerance and grain development. QTL analysis identified 13 genomic regions associated with post-flowering drought tolerance measures.

The DEGs common to both genotypes under salt stress are key genes for quinoa to adapt to salinity. These candidate genes regulate salt tolerance by participating in ROS scavenging, protein kinases biosynthesis, and plant hormone signal transduction.

A population of 96 F2:7 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) was developed from a cross between BTx623 and PI 607900. Critical RILs and new molecular markers were used for fine mapping, identifying a major resistance QTL named SgR1 (Schizaphis graminum Resistance 1). SgR1 was narrowed from a 508 kb region on chromosome 9 to an 82.7-kb interval flanked by markers GSR345 and GSR348.

text: Based on three Striga resistance indices, 25 landraces exhibited greater resistance than the resistant check variety Berhan. The most resistant landraces included ETSL_100874, ETSL_101451, ETSL_101210, ETSL_100275, ETSL_100494, IS_38279, and ETSL_101258.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) has a short growth period, drought tolerance, and barren tolerance. Exploration of its dwarfing mechanism and dwarf genes can provide a reference for dwarf breeding of other C4 crops.

Overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana showed that the CC, CC-NBS, and CC-LRR fragments of PMTR1 induce cell death. In PMTR3, the CC and full-length fragments induce cell death. Luciferase complementation imaging and pull-down assays revealed distinct interaction activities between the CC and NBS fragments.

Transcriptome and metabolomic analyses showed differentially expressed genes in the resistant variety HN16 and susceptible variety BTX623 were enriched in the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway. Differentially expressed metabolites were related to isoflavonoid biosynthesis and flavonoid biosynthesis. q-PCR results of key genes were consistent with transcriptome expression results.

The BC1F6 genotypes were highly resistant and similar to 'Honey Drip'. This resistance QTL can be introgressed into many sorghum genotypes using marker assisted selection, conferring resistance to M. incognita.
ML-GWAS using 127,804 SNP markers identified 49 stable quantitative trait nucleotides. Candidate genes included ALMT for root length, an F-BOX domain for root angle, and FAR1 for root hair number.

Twenty-two QTLs and four clusters were detected. Five stable QTLs are co-located on chromosome 3H. They are Qgna.sau-3H for grain N concentration, Qtna.sau-3H for total N accumulation per plant, Qnhi.sau-3H for N harvest index, Qnutegy.sau-3H for N utilization efficiency for grain yield, and Qanutedm.sau-3H.1 for N utilization efficiency for aboveground dry matter. They are flanked by markers bpb6282426 and bpb4786261.

text: The dwarfing gene sdw1 has been used to develop commercial barley varieties. There are at least four different alleles at the sdw1 locus. Mutations in the gibberellin 20-oxidase gene (HvGA20ox2) resulted in multiple alleles at the sdw1 locus.

We screened germplasm and obtained purple plant varieties in foxtail millet. The purple-leaved variety B100 and the green-leaved control Yugu1 were studied. B100 accumulates anthocyanin in the leaf epidermis and has stronger stress tolerance.

A QTL on chromosome 2 was stable across environments, had positive additive effects, explained phenotypic variance, and was associated with the Psy3 gene. This is the first report of a QTL for carotenoid content in sorghum.

Transgenic studies of three genes from the locus showed that Sobic.006G061100 (SbSNF4-2) increased BM, SSC, JW, and PH when overexpressed in sorghum and sugarcane, and delayed flowering in transgenic sorghum. SbSNF4-2 encodes a gamma subunit of the AMPK/SNF1/SnRK1 heterotrimeric complexes. Overexpression of human or mouse gamma 2 in heart or the whole body also increases heart or body biomass.

Quantitative trait loci (QTL) mapping identified 22 QTL for nine panicle-related traits. The increased effect alleles of 16 QTL were derived from the parent Changsheng07. Seven QTL (qMPL3.1, qMPL5, qMPW2, qSD5, qTGW5.1, qTGW5.2 and qGL5) were major QTL, explaining phenotypic variation of 11.41%, 12.78%, 13.75%, 34.81%, 50.14%, 40.28% and 11.32%, respectively.

QTL mapping identified genomic regions for tiller development. GWAS provided insights into genetic variations. Transcriptome analyses revealed gene expression and regulatory mechanisms for tillering. Integration of these approaches identified key genetic loci and expression patterns.

Oats regulated Ca2+ concentration through CAX and ACA after salt stress. Oats decoded Ca2+ signals through CML and transferred them to downstream receptors via the Ca2+ sensors CaM and CDPK. This activated K+/Na+ transporters such as SOS1 and NHX.

Ribosomes expression is down-regulated, while HSPs and CRT expression is up-regulated, in response to salt stress through protein synthesis regulation. However, GABA metabolism shows a different synthesis pattern in Bai5 and Vao-9.

The spring six-row cross Steptoe (dormant)/Morex (non-dormant) doubled haploid mapping population was evaluated in greenhouse and field environments. QTL regions for SSc, AA, and GP were revealed on five, four, and six of the seven barley chromosomes, respectively. Seven and eight regions on five and six chromosomes had effects ranging from 4 to 31% on PHS and 3 to 39% on dormancy, respectively.

text: The study identified and validated quantitative trait loci (QTL) for grain yield and other agronomic traits using phenotypic data from three post-rainy dry sorghum seasons. The mapping population had 245 F-9 recombinant inbred lines derived from a cross of M35-1 x B35. A genetic linkage map was constructed with 237 markers including 174 genomic, 60 genic and 3 morphological markers.

Two P5CS genes, SbP5CS1 (GenBank GQ377719) and SbP5CS2 (GenBank GQ377720), were isolated from sweet sorghum. SbP5CS1 and SbP5CS2 are located on chromosome 3 and chromosome 9, respectively. They encode 729 and 716 amino acid polypeptides, respectively. The homology between SbP5CS1 and SbP5CS2 is 76%.

A high-density genetic map was constructed using SLAF-seq technology with a population of 134 RILs from a cross between Golden Promise (GP) and H602. The map contained 12,635 SLAFs with 26,693 SNPs and spanned 896.74 cM on seven chromosomes. Based on this map, 16 QTLs for grain length (GL), grain width and thousand-grain weight were detected on chromosomes 1H, 2H, 4H, 5H, and 6H.

We evaluated nine grain size and weight traits in a double haploid population for three years. Using five mapping methods, we identified 60 reliable QTLs and 27 hotspot regions distributed on all chromosomes except 6H. These QTLs control the nine grain size and weight traits.

Overexpression of SiMYB56 upregulates ABA synthesis and response genes under drought. SiMYB56 enhances drought resistance in transgenic rice by regulating lignin biosynthesis and the ABA signaling pathway. SiMYB56 is a candidate gene for drought resistance improvement in gramineous crops.
21 QTL for FLL and 28 QTL for FLW were identified on seven chromosomes of qingke. 10 QTL were stable, detected in at least 2 environments, with PVE of 8.41%-26.36%.

Drought stress did not significantly change chlorophyll content, but it significantly reduced net photosynthetic rate and other photosynthetic parameters while increasing proline content. Transcriptome analysis of seedlings from control and treatment groups identified 344 differentially expressed genes.
Chloroplastic beta-amylase 1 gene LOC8070506 and alpha-glucosidase gene LOC8061607 levels increased in both cultivars under drought conditions. Their levels were higher in SCS than in JL.
Genetic markers associated with seven RSA traits were identified in 182 Ethiopian sorghum landraces using multi locus GWAS and SNP markers. The germplasm exhibited phenotypic variation.
Qsd1 and Qsd2 have significant genetic effects on seed dormancy in barley. The two KASP markers can be used in breeding to select favourable alleles for seed dormancy duration.

Sorghum (Sorghum bicolor L. Moench) withstands light frost during juvenile development. This frost tolerance trait allows earlier sowing, prolongs the growing season and maturity, and increases yield potential.

The dehydration-tolerant cultivars IC-403579 and Prasad showed lower lipid peroxidation and electrolyte leakage than the dehydration-sensitive cultivars IC-480117 and Lepakshi. Tolerant genotypes maintained higher activity of catalase, ascorbate peroxidase, and glutathione reductase during polyethylene glycol treatments compared to sensitive ones.

Sobic.003G350800 gene expression was higher in leaf, root, flower, panicle, and stem. Sobic.005G188300 and Sobic.001G463800 were moderately expressed at grain maturity and anthesis in leaf, root, panicle, and seed.

Only about 7% of surveyed landraces carried natural dwarfing alleles, with dw3 locus mutations most frequent. Plant height and F2 genotyping, from parents 8R252 (tall) and 8R387 (dwarf), showed dw3 had the most pronounced effect on height reduction.

Genomic selection (GS), quantitative trait loci (QTL) mapping, marker-assisted-selection (MAS), genome-wide association studies (GWAS), and CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing help pinpoint and activate resistance genes. CRISPR/Cas9 allows precise modifications that can trigger plant defence mechanisms.

Fourteen QTL for HD were identified. HD gene expression varied with TOS, indicating QTLxenvironment interaction. Eleven of the fourteen QTL were located at positions similar to previously mapped HD genes.

RNA-seq was used to examine differentially expressed genes in alkali-tolerant Z14 to reveal the molecular mechanism of sorghum response to alkali stress. Plant height, root length, and biomass of both cultivars decreased under 80 mM NaHCO3 treatment, but Z14 showed better water retention.

19 polymorphic markers detected 98 alleles with an average of 4.9 alleles per locus. Observed heterozygosity ranged from 0.1 to 0.5, with a mean of 0.42 per locus. The markers are useful for gene tagging, diversity analyses and marker assisted selection in mung bean.

Subcellular localization analysis showed the SiMYBS3 protein was located in the nucleus and cytoplasm. Transactivation assay showed SiMYBS3 had transcriptional activation activity in yeast. Overexpression of SiMYBS3 in Arabidopsis thaliana conferred drought tolerance, ABA insensitivity, and earlier flowering.

QTL analysis identified 45 major main-effect QTLs for 21 traits. Three QTL clusters (Cluster-1-Ah03, Cluster-2-Ah12, Cluster-3-Ah20) harbor more than half of the major QTLs (30/45) for various heat tolerant traits. These clusters explain 10.4%-38.6%, 10.6%-44.6%, and 10.1%-49.5% of phenotypic variance, respectively.
A proteomic analysis used two foxtail millet varieties: JK3 (tolerant) and B175 (sensitive). Leaf samples were collected at 0 h, 12 h, and 24 h after saline-alkali stress.
Several markers influence multiple traits, suggesting pleiotropy or linkage. Integrating QTLs and GWAS results for yield and drought traits identified overlapping regions and candidate genes.

The saline-alkali tolerance of 524 oat germplasm resources was evaluated using culture-dish germination and field identification methods. Saline-alkali stress inhibited the germination potential, germination rate, and shoot and root lengths during the germination period.

Tartary buckwheat varieties Chuanqiao No. 1 (salt-tolerant) and Chuanqiao No. 2 (salt-sensitive) were used. ABA concentrations were applied under salt stress. Optimal ABA concentrations were 30 mu M for Chuanqiao No. 1 and 20 mu M for Chuanqiao No. 2.
ABA-mediated regulation in quinoa was characterized. Combined stress triggered ABA accumulation by up-regulating NCED - ABA biosynthesis genes and suppressing CYP707A - ABA catabolism genes.

Real-time PCR analysis showed that 29 SiCDPK genes were induced under drought and ABA stresses. SiCDPK24 had the highest expression levels at 6 and 12 hours of drought treatment and was selected for further analysis.
Quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa) has been introduced into China. Many varieties have been developed through selection, but few through mutagenesis breeding.

This marker identifies barley chasmogamous lines with large GOA. The two minor QTLs were validated at three locations across two seasons after removing DH lines carrying the candidate gene Cly1 of QGOA-2H-2.

A quantitative trait locus (QTLs) detection for traits related to drought tolerance benefits isolation of drought-responsive genetic elements and marker-assisted selection breeding. A full-coverage genetic linkage map was developed based on simple sequence repeat (SSR) markers using a recombinant inbred line (RIL) population derived from an intra-specific cross between two drought-resistant varieties. The map was anchored with 313 markers.

CRISPR/Cas9 targeted modification of susceptibility genes in rice, tomato, wheat, and citrus is reviewed for resistance to fungal and bacterial diseases. This genome editing develops crop plants resistant to specific pests and pathogens.

text: Composite interval mapping was used on each population, identifying 105 QTLs. These QTLs were projected onto a combined consensus map, resulting in 25 meta QTLs for seven traits.
Two novel QTLs were identified: qSL2-1 on chromosome 2H and qSL5-1 on chromosome 5H. The qSL2-1 is 0.06 Mb and contains three high-confidence genes.

FeR2R3-MYB was cloned from the anthocyanin-rich common buckwheat variety 'QZZTQ'. FeR2R3-MYB contains two MYB DNA-binding domains and is located in the nucleus with transcriptional activation activity.
22.8% of these MQTL had overlapping CIs. The overlapping regions were mainly on chromosomes 1H, 2H and 4H.
A panel of 333 sorghum lines was studied for leaf senescence onset and progression. The progression of leaf senescence significantly correlated with variations in final leaf greenness.
The SiLTP promoter drove GUS expression in transgenic Arabidopsis. SiLTP expression was induced by NaCl, polyethylene glycol, and abscisic acid (ABA).

Pc39 was mapped in two RIL populations: AC Assiniboia/MN841801 and AC Medallion/MN841801. SNP markers for Pc39 postulation in oat germplasm were identified. Pc39 was mapped to a linkage group with 16 SNP markers, placing the gene on linkage group Mrg11 (chromosome 1C) of the oat consensus map.

The F-2 mapping population 'Av1860' x 'Fuchs' was analyzed using DArTseq methodology to identify dominant silicoDArT markers for the Pm4 gene. Among 46,230 silicoDArT markers, 126 markers were highly correlated with powdery mildew resistance conditioned by the Pm4 gene.

Evidence for multiple copies of the FL int2 sequence was found in both the A and C genome groups. The A genome copies showed greater divergence than the C genome sequences. Phylogenetic analysis of the FL int2 sequences resulted in four major groups, reemphasizing the major genomic divergence between the A and C genomes and the close relationship among the A, B, and D genomes.

90% of 168 evaluated orthologous genes were conserved across species for drought tolerance. Over 50% of identified maize and rice genes were responsive to drought and salt stresses and were co-located within multifunctional QTLs.

Genes involved in photosynthesis and the antioxidant system were activated under alkali stress. In stress signal transduction, Ca2+ signal transduction pathway-related genes were activated, about 23 PP2Cs in ABA signaling were upregulated, and multiple MAPK and other kinase-related genes were triggered by alkali stress. This helps decipher the response mechanism of sorghum to alkali stress and improve its alkali tolerance.

text: A population of 188 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) from a cross between grain sorghum M71 and sweet sorghum SS79 was used. A genetic map with 157 AFLP, SSR, and EST-SSR markers was constructed. QTLs associated with sugar components (Brix, glucose, sucrose, total sugar content) and sugar-related agronomic traits (flowering date, plant height, stem diameter, tiller number per plant, fresh panicle weight, estimated juice weight) were detected in four environments using composite interval mapping (CIM).

Among potential drought-responsive genes in XZSN, 1,206 and 1,274 genes were identified as potential positive and negative contributors for drought resistance compared to LK3. Furthermore, 851 out of 1,206 positive drought-resistant genes were identified as core drought-resistant genes in XZSN based on WGCNA analysis, and most were induced earlier and quicker by drought stress than in LK3.
Root system architecture plays an important role in plant adaptation to drought stress. The genetic basis of RSA in sorghum is not fully elucidated.

Genomic regions controlling germination and early seedling drought tolerance in Setaria were identified using 190 F-7 lines from a cross between Yugu1, a S. italica cultivar, and a wild S. viridis genotype. Quantitative trait loci were identified for traits including promptness index, radical root length, coleoptile length, lateral root number at germinating stage, and seedling survival rate after rehydration.

text: VERNALIZATION1 (VRN1) regulates the vernalization requirement. It affects the yield and geographical distribution of Triticeae crops by regulating flowering time. The first intron (Intron I) of VRN1 contains cis-elements that repress VRN1 expression. Allelic variations in Intron I are associated with increased VRN1 expression in vernalization-responsive accessions without vernalization.
An OAT gene AtOAT from Arabidopsis was expressed in wheat. AtOAT expression enhanced drought and salt stress tolerance in wheat by increasing proline content and peroxidase activity.
The costs of C-MAB were estimated. Lines with stay-green trait and good performance were identified at BC2.

Six DEGs were identified within the qGS1 region by integrating fine mapping intervals and RNA-seq data. Quantitative real-time PCR confirmed that six genes exhibited different expression at two stages. Bioinformatics analysis showed Sobic.001G230700 is the most likely candidate gene for the qGS1 locus.

SAP lines were evaluated for SA resistance in the field during the 2019 and 2020 growing seasons in Tifton, GA. In 2020, SAP accessions were also evaluated for SA resistance in the field using drone-based high throughput phenotyping.
The barley mutant allele sdw3 confers a gibberellin-insensitive, semi-dwarf phenotype. sdw3 was delimited to a 0.04 cM interval in the proximal region of the short arm of barley chromosome 2H.
Drought-related QTL were on linkage groups 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, and 10. Two maturity QTL mapped on linkage groups 7 and 8 separately.

A total of 23 QTLs for 15 traits were identified using a high-density Bin map with 3024 Bin markers. Among them, qPH9 was a major QTL for plant height with a large phenotypic variation explained (51.6%).

Under drought stress, auxin, cytokinin, and brassinosteroid signaling pathways were suppressed in Longyan 3. Abscisic acid and jasmonic acid signaling pathways were activated in DA92-2F6. Upregulated PP2C, ABF, SNRK2, GID1, JAZ, and MYC2 genes may enhance drought tolerance in DA92-2F6.

Two barley populations, W89001002003 x I60049 (W x I, six-row type) and Merit x H93174006 (M x H, two-row type), contained 200 and 127 recombinant inbred lines (RILs). The RILs were phenotyped for leaf Delta C-13 and agronomic traits under rain-fed environments. A transgressive segregation pattern for leaf Delta C-13 was observed among the RILs.

text: Drought stress impacts glucose metabolism in quinoa seeds. Candidate drought tolerance genes in the glucose metabolism pathway were verified by qRT-PCR and yeast expression.

Individual families were genotyped, and early-season field performance was measured for two years. The SQR allele of Xtxp43 had favorable effects on seedling vigor in both populations and on emergence in the Tx2794 population.
The tan2-d allele has a C-to-T transition causing a premature stop codon before the bHLH domain in Tannin2. KASP assays targeting these recessive alleles were developed to genotype large populations.

The relative abundance of endophytic Acremonium persicinum (OTU5) increases under drought. This co-occurs with an increase in fungal membrane dynamics and plant metabolites, including ethanolamine. There is a negative association between endophytic A. persicinum (OTU5) and plant pathogenic fungi. This co-occurs with increased expression of the endophyte's biosynthetic gene clusters.
QTLs for root volume, root fresh weight, and root dry weight co-localized on chromosome SBI-04. These traits are positively correlated and can be improved using the same linked markers.
Of the 1710 SSR markers, 1692 were mapped onto ten sorghum chromosomes by in silico mapping. Each chromosome had from 107 to 285 markers.

The MAT approach edited the phytoene desaturase gene. A high-throughput technique and a novel method were developed to identify single-copy transformed plants and determine transgene independent integration.
FtMYB10 overexpression in Arabidopsis reduced drought and salt tolerance. The transgenic plants had decreased proline contents and reduced P5CS1 gene expression under normal and stress conditions.

A weighted gene co-expression network analysis of 17,424 highly expressed genes uncovered black and turquoise modules. The black module has two hub genes belonging to the UGT family. The turquoise module has three hub genes belonging to the phenolics pathway. These modules are significantly associated with the variation of phenolic metabolites. The results reveal changes of phenolic metabolites during grain development under drought stress.

A QTL (ORSratio(IX_230)) related to root shoot ratio (TRT) was detected. It explained 27.6% and 28.9% phenotypic variance in two seasons. The QTL was close to the SNP marker TP6337 (MRK) and flanked by SNPs TP518 (MRK) and TP1280 (MRK).

Analysis of cis-acting elements showed promoters of saline-alkaline stress-inducible ERF genes contain LTRs, DREs, MYBs, ABREs, MYCs, CGTCA- and TGACG-motifs, involved in abiotic stress responses. qRT-PCR determined expression of VaERF3 was induced by NaHCO3, polyethylene glycol 6000, NaCl, and ABA.

The study identified six SiEUL gene family members in foxtail millet. Analysis covered their properties, evolution, protein structures, chromosomal locations, promoter cis-elements, and transcriptional activity across tissues and developmental stages.

Quinoa accessions show variation in epidermal bladder cell (EBC) density and salt tolerance (ST). Two accessions with contrasting ST and EBC density, Q30 (low ST, high EBC) and Q68 (high ST, low EBC), were studied.

After drought, salt, and abscisic acid treatment, SbANK56 showed the highest expression among family members. Overexpression of SbANK56 improved survival rate compared to wild type under drought. SbANK56 overexpressing plants had lower malondialdehyde and higher proline contents than wild type under drought.

The closest flanking markers were approximately 6 and 14 cM away from the resistance locus in an Fg population. These markers are useful for discriminating resistant germplasm through marker-assisted selection and represent the first step towards map-based cloning of the resistance gene.
Microsatellite SSR markers were developed for germplasm evaluation. 500 novel EST-based and genomic SSRs were developed from mung bean transcriptome and genome sequences.

Rphq2 candidate genes encode peroxidases, kinases and a seven-in-absentia protein family member. Rphq2 is not an NB-LRR gene family member and does not resemble previously cloned partial resistance genes.

A high density genetic map was constructed. 10598 SSR markers were developed from the foxtail millet cultivar 'Yugu1' genome. 1013 polymorphic SSR markers between Yugu1 and Longgu7 were used to genotype 167 individuals from a Yugu1 x Longgu7 F-2 population.

A OIL-hotspot genomic region was identified, related to drought tolerance traits: dry root biomass, root surface area, lateral root number, dry shoot biomass and shoot length. A OIL (ORSratio(IX_230)) was detected, related to root shoot ratio, an important drought avoidance trait, explaining the highest phenotypic variance of 27.6% and 28.9% in two consecutive seasons.

Metabolic and transcriptional analysis were used to study grain filling in foxtail millet. 2104 known metabolites were identified during grain filling. Functional analysis revealed stage-specific metabolic properties in foxtail millet grain filling.

Fengtian had higher contents of K+, NO3-, soluble sugar, and betaine in leaf blades and higher contributions of these osmolytes to leaf osmotic potential than Lvjuren. The upregulated expressions of SbAKT1, SbHAK5, SbSKOR, SbNPF3.1, SbNPF6.3, and SbNPF7.3 maintained K+ and NO3- homeostasis under NaCl treatment.
BSR-resistant adzuki bean lines have been screened in pathogen-infected fields. However, field selection using pathogen inoculation is time-consuming and labor-intensive.

This study assessed multi-trait models to improve genomic prediction of parental breeding values for grain yield and stay-green in sorghum using correlated auxiliary traits. It determined trait combinations that optimize predictive results. The dataset included phenotypic performance of 2645 testcross hybrids across 26 environments, and genomic and pedigree information on their female parental lines.

A genome-wide association study (GWAS) of sweet sorghum [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench] was performed to find genetic loci associated with single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) markers and explore drought-tolerance candidate genes. The study used the general linear model (GLM), mixed linear model (MLM) and the fixed and random model circulating probability unification (FarmCPU) method in R.

text: 142 ANK genes of sorghum were identified and classified into 12 subfamilies. The promoter region of SbANK genes contains stress-responsive elements.

Eight candidate accessions were identified with high genomic estimated breeding values across nine phenotypes using SNP markers. Four candidate accessions were identified with high genomic estimated breeding values across 22 phenotypes using SV markers.

The study identified QTLs for post-flowering drought tolerance (stay green), pre-flowering drought tolerance, and lodging tolerance in sorghum. It used an F-7 recombinant inbred line (RIL) population derived from the cross SC56xTx7000. The RIL lines and parents were evaluated for these traits in multiple environments.

text: Three key pathways (starch and sucrose metabolism, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, and phytohormone signaling) are pivotal in the drought response during seed germination in sorghum. Most drought tolerance-related genes in these pathways were expressed at higher levels in the drought-tolerant cultivar W069.

Candidate genes VRADI07G24840, VRADI07G17780, and VRADI07G15650 regulate trichome density on the dorsal leaf surface of mungbean. VRADI07G24840 encodes ZFP6, VRADI07G17780 encodes a bHLH DNA-binding superfamily protein, and VRADI07G15650 encodes MYB102. Candidate genes VRADIO7G29860, VRADIO7G29450, and VRADIO7G28520 have roles in chlorophyll biosynthesis. VRADIO7G29860 encodes s-adenosyl-L-methionine, VRADIO7G29450 encodes FTSHI1 protein, and VRADIO7G28520 encodes CRS2-associated factor.
We detected 17 QTL, 10 at adult plant stage, 5 at seedling stage, and 2 shared across stages. The strongest signal was a four-peak cluster on chromosome 1A (370.8-464.4 Mb) within the Pg13 interval.

Transcriptome analysis at the seedling stage revealed that cultivars JRK3 and JRK6 regulated phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, MAPK signaling, and plant hormone signal transduction in response to drought stress. Cultivars JKH4 and JKH6 regulated fatty acid elongation in response to drought stress.
The study conducted association mapping of drought tolerance with seedling and yield traits in cowpeas. 60 cowpea accessions were used. SNPs were discovered through genotyping-by-sequencing.

78 PgAP2/ERF members were identified in the P. glaucum genome and classified into AP2, ERF, DREB, RAV, and soloist subfamilies. The study included phylogenetic analysis, gene structure, conserved motifs, chromosomal localization, gene duplication, expression pattern, protein interaction network, and functional characterization of PgRAV_01.
Sequencing generated 977 million reads, which were assembled into contigs. 11,627 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified in both lines.

The grain HIS content of Schooner No. 3 was 0.21 mg/g. The grain HIS content of the population ranged from 0.23 to 0.54 mg/g. Genetic linkage maps for seven barley chromosomes were constructed using 180 SSR markers. The total genetic distance was 2671.03 cM with an average marker spacing of 14.84 cM. QTL analysis of grain HIS content in barley was performed using IciMappingV3.3. Three QTLs were detected.

Sorghum seedlings were exposed to CdCl2 concentrations of 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, and 150 μM. Cd stress decreased peroxidase (POD), superoxide dismutase (SOD), glutathione S-transferase (GST), and catalase (CAT) activities. It increased malondialdehyde (MDA) levels. This inhibited plant height, lateral root density, and plant biomass production.

Transgenic rice expressing SiMYB16 (OX16) had higher survival rate, lower malondialdehyde content, and heavier fresh weight than wild-type (WT) under salt stress. The transgenic plants also had higher germination rate under salt treatment and higher yield in the field compared to wild-type plants. Transcriptome analysis showed that up-regulated genes in the transgenic rice were involved in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, fatty acid elongation, phenylalanine metabolism, and flavonoid biosynthesis pathways.

Major QTLs were identified for late flowering and perenniality traits. Late flowering QTLs (6, PVE 11.2-29.9%) are from ACC 1. Perenniality QTLs (3, PVE 17.4-22.6%) are from ACC 87. Across four mungbean populations, 18 congruent QTLs were found for 13 traits.
Jasmonic acid levels increased under GB infestation. Salicylic acid levels increased at 15 DAI in YSA-infested plants. Metabolite levels were altered by aphid infestation.

Low temperature at the seedling stage adversely affects sorghum growth and development. APETALA2/Ethylene-Responsive transcription factors (AP2/ERFs) play essential roles in growth, development, and responses to abiotic stresses.

Transgenic rice plants had a higher seed germination rate and higher percentage seedling survival than wild-type plants under prolonged salt stress. In vivo and in situ assays revealed that the accumulation of reactive oxygen species in transgenic rice plants was significantly lower than that in wild-type plants.

Bulked segregant analysis used progeny lines from a cross between Cascade and AC Marie. It identified randomly amplified polymorphic DNA markers linked to genetic factors controlling primary kernel hull percentage in oat. Twelve polymorphisms were tested for linkage to hull percentage by genotyping 80 F-2-derived F-8 lines.

The sdw1.d allele from Diamant has a 7-bp deletion in exon 1. The sdw1.c allele from Abed Denso has a 1-bp deletion and a 4-bp insertion in the 5' UTR. The sdw1.a allele from Jotun results from a total deletion of the HvGA20ox2 gene. These changes cause lower gene expression in sdw1.d and no expression in sdw1.a.
An had a high soluble sugar to starch ratio. YG had a consistent ratio. RNA-seq showed lower expression of beta-amylase 2 in YG than in An.

Conventional breeding approaches like pure line selection, pedigree selection, mutagenesis, wide crosses and polyploidy have been used to develop oat varieties. Molecular approaches like molecular markers, QTL mapping, genome-wide association studies, genetic engineering, genomic selection and speed breeding are utilized to identify traits or genes, produce plants with desired agronomic traits, and accelerate genetic gain.

Expression analysis showed that SbP5CS1 and SbP5CS2 transcripts were up-regulated in 10-day-old sweet sorghum seedlings after drought, salt (250 mM NaCl), and MeJA (10 mu M) treatment. The expression levels of both SbP5CS genes increased significantly after 3-day drought stress. Under high salt treatment, peak SbP5CS1 expression was at 4 h and SbP5CS2 at 8 h in roots, while expression trends in leaves were nearly identical.
Drought stress is an abiotic factor affecting common buckwheat (Fagopyrum esculentum). A proteomic analysis monitored proteomic variations under drought stress in the seedling stage.

Quantitative trait loci (QTLs) for flowering time (FT) in lentil were located using a recombinant inbred line population derived from the intraspecific cross Precoz x WA8649041. FT experiments were conducted in Haymana, Turkey and Pullman, WA, USA from 1998 to 2002.

PH data and BLUP variations were collected from nine environments and three development stages.

Zhangzagu 13 was used to examine the effects of zinc fertilizer application methods on foxtail millet quality and flavonoid biosynthesis. Transcriptome and metabolome analyses were used.

Classic phenotype-based selection methods can be integrated with modern -omics in sorghum breeding programs to address salt tolerance and production. We explore correlations between sorghum performance indices under salt stress at phenotypic and genotypic levels for genetic improvement.
Aco245 is a diagnostic marker for MAS of Dw6. Dw6 NILs are used to study vacuolar proton ATPases in plant growth and development.

A genome-wide association study identified 29 reliable markers, including 19 novel markers. Four markers were associated with shoot transpiration efficiency (STE). Genotypes ETSC17129-6-1 and ETSC17300-4-1 exhibited high STE and carried favorable alleles at all four loci.

text: Transgenic Arabidopsis lines with the FtbHLH2 promoter had higher GUS activity after cold stress. This suggests that FtbHLH2 may positively regulate cold stress in Tartary buckwheat.

These regions were involved in genes controlling stomata length (LR), leaf number (LN), leaf weight (LW), and genetic score (SCR). Three major stable pleiotropic QTLs (qSCS-1, qRLS-1, and qLNN-1) were associated with SCR, root length (RL), and root number (RN) in both normal and salinity treatments. Two major stable pleiotropic QTLs (qSNN-3 and qLWS-3) associated with stomata number (SN) and LW appeared promising for marker-assisted selection (MAS).

363 differentially expressed genes were obtained in adzuki bean seedling unifoliate leaves treated with 32.5 mmol/L NaCl. 858 differentially expressed genes were obtained with 65.0 mmol/L NaCl. Up-regulated genes enriched in the spliceosome pathway. Down-regulated genes enriched in plant hormone signal transduction, plant-pathogen interaction, and MAPK signaling pathway.
Three QTL for resistance to leaf blast were identified on chromosomes 1, 2, and 8 of the foxtail millet cultivar Yugu 5. Leaf blast disease of foxtail millet is caused by Pyricularia spp.

text: Three bulk samples were made: Y-bulk (early-heading), S-bulk (late-heading), and L-bulk (extremely late-heading). Genome-wide SNP comparison of Y-bulk vs. S-bulk and Y-bulk vs. L-bulk identified two QTLs associated with DTH.

A barley DH population was evaluated for 13 stem traits after 28 days of heading in two consecutive years. Lodging index (LI) correlated with other stem traits. Using experimental and BLUP data, three mapping methods detected 27 stable major QTLs and 22 QTL clustered regions.

Barley yield is limited by low rainfall and high temperature during the growing season. Carbon isotope discrimination (ΔC-13) is an index for selecting stable yielding crops in rain-fed environments. Identification of quantitative trait loci (QTL) and linked markers for ΔC-13 will enhance its use in breeding programs.
126 silicoDArT markers correlated with resistance to powdery mildew in oat conditioned by the Pm4 gene. 48 sequences were chosen for potential conversion into specific STS markers.

Participants were willing to pay a premium for rice with a 'no fungicide' attribute, which cisgenics and GM can provide. 76% and 73% of respondents stated a willingness to consume GM and cisgenic foods, respectively.
Fifteen markers in our study were on the same linkage groups as QTL from previous studies. Four markers colocalized within 4 cM of three previously detected QTL.
The upland switchgrass cultivar Summer is a host for greenbug aphids and yellow sugarcane aphids. The lowland cultivar Kanlow exhibits multi-species resistance that curtails aphid reproduction.

Sorghum species LRNK1 (salt-tolerant) and LR2381 (salt-sensitive) were treated with 180 mM NaCl. Physiological indicators were measured. Transcriptomic analysis was performed by Illumina sequencing. Metabolomic analysis was performed by LC-MS.

Q77 was the control. Proline content, CAT activity, soluble sugar content and MDA content were measured in three drought-resistant and four non-drought-resistant quinoa varieties at different growth stages. Yield and quality were determined after grain harvest.
The study identified genomic regions influencing root and shoot traits during the seedling phase. It used 129 sorghum accessions from Ethiopia, showing significant genotype variation for all traits.

VfAP2-1 and VfERF-99 significantly increased chlorophyll a, carotenoids, and total chlorophyll content, suggesting roles in promoting photosynthesis. The study analyzed VfAP2/ERFs and explored VfAP2-1 and VfERF-99 functions in biotic and abiotic stresses and photosynthesis.

Transgenic plants ectopically expressing FtWRKY46 had significantly higher seed germination rate, root length, chlorophyll content, and proline content than WT plants after salt stress. They also had significantly lower MDA levels. Salt treatment increased the expression of stress-related genes.

Inactivation of photosynthesis in pea, including dark and light reactions, was connected with VP. Inactivation of dark reactions decreased the rate constant of the fast relaxation of the electrochromic pigment absorbance shift, reflecting a decrease in H(+)-ATP synthase activity.
32 putative CIPK genes (SbCIPKs) were identified in sweet sorghum [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench].

Yield-related and morphological traits were evaluated under drought-stressed (DS) and well-watered (WW) conditions in F-3 and F-4 segregating generations derived from a cross between two genotypes S19-3 and DodR. In the F-3 segregating population, significant QTLs for shoot dry weight (SDW) were mapped on LG10 accounting for 15.5% PVE under well-watered conditions. A putative QTL for SDW was mapped on LG10 with 10.10% PVE under drought-stressed conditions.

Genome comparison using the qingke reference revealed a close genetic relationship between hulled barley cv. Morex and hulless barley cv. Zangqing320. The relationship is supported by low-diversity regions in the two genomes. Considering Morex's breeding pedigree origin, a close genomic relationship exists between modern cultivated barley and qingke.
Sorghum uses less water than rice, maize, and wheat. Sorghum uses up to 34% less water than rice in rainfed systems and up to 50% less under irrigation.
The NAC transcription factor family is a plant-specific regulatory gene family. Its role in foxtail millet's defense against pathogens is unexplored.

Drought stress reduced water and chlorophyll content, and increased Malondialdehyde (MDA) level and soluble protein in foxtail millet leaves. The drought resistance order was Jigu39, Jingu21, and Longgu16.

Trait correlation analysis showed that leaf senescence progression significantly correlated with final leaf greenness variations. GWAS identified 31 senescence-associated genomic regions containing 148 genes, of which 124 were related to leaf senescence progression.
An elite oat panel of 378 genotypes was screened for RKN resistance. Genome-wide association analyses were performed using nematode reproduction factor.

SbWRKY51 expression was up-regulated by salt stress, drought, and abscisic acid (ABA) in sorghum. Overexpression of SbWRKY51 via Agrobacterium rhizogenes-mediated transformation enhanced salt tolerance, resulting in longer root length, increased biomass, less Na+ content, and higher antioxidant enzyme activities compared to wild-type plants.

The mapping population comprises 155 F-2 lines from a cross between Kamphaeng Saen 1 and V4718. It was analyzed with 67 SSR markers. The F2:3 families were evaluated for 100-seed weight and days to flowering in 2008 and 2011.
SiHAK1 transport activity was modulated by external K+ supply and internal K+ content under low K and salt stress. It mediates K homeostasis in S. italica under K+ deficiency and salt stress.
GA(3) and kinetin increased photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance, and transpiration rate. Salicylic acid reduced membrane permeability and Na+ content under salt stress.

SiEULS3 was strongly expressed under osmotic stress and regulated by hormones. Over-expression of SiEULS3 in Arabidopsis enhanced osmotic and drought tolerance by improving antioxidant capacity and hydration.

Most QTL were the same. One QTL for panicle length, two QTL for 1000 seed weight, and one QTL for panicle diameter were different. Few QTL differed between seasons, suggesting gene expression varies under different conditions.

The SiNRX1 gene in foxtail millet was knocked out using CRISPR/Cas9. The drought resistance of sinrx1 mutants was identified at the germination and seedling stages. Transcriptome sequencing and DIA quantitative proteomics of sinrx1 mutants and wild types under drought and control conditions at the seedling stage were performed to analyze the molecular mechanism of SiNRX1 regulating drought resistance.

Under drought and high salt stress, SbP5CS1 expression was higher than SbP5CS2. Under MeJA treatment, SbP5CS1 transcript increased at 2 h in leaves, and SbP5CS2 expression increased at 4 h. SbP5CS1 and SbP5CS2 show different temporal and spatial expression patterns.

We confirmed QTLs for pod length (qPoL8) and 100-seed weight (qSW8) on chromosome 8, and for seed number per pod (qSN9.2) on chromosome 9. Some QTLs for these traits were clustered on chromosomes 5, 7, 8, 9 and 10.

Four TLS resistance QTL were identified. Two QTL were in each population. Three QTL were previously unidentified. A major QTL on chromosome 5 in the BTx623/BTx642 RIL population corresponded to the previously identified TLS resistance gene ds1.

53 QTLs for six traits and their salt tolerance index (STI) were detected under both conditions. Phenotypic variation explained (PVE) ranged from 4.16% to 20.42%. Six QTLs (qTB6, qSFW9, qJW9, qBrix2, qBrix10, qSTI-Brix9) were main effect QTLs controlling salt tolerance with PVE > 10%. qSFW9 and qJW9 colocalized in the marker interval SB5069-UGSM18. Their PVEs were 17.70% and 14.20%, respectively, with positive effects from L-Tian.

text: The promoters of AsTCP genes contain cis-acting elements for hormone response, abiotic stress, light response, and growth and development. The oat TCP gene family is mainly amplified by fragment duplication. Two tandem duplications, AsTCP058 / AsTCP059 and AsTCP023 / AsTCP025, are stably present in three oats.

CcCIPK14-overexpressed plants with reduced CcCBL1 transcript levels had lower survival rates, indicating CcCBL1 is in the same pathway as CcCIPK14. The CcCIPK14-CcCBL1 complex regulates flavonoid biosynthesis in pigeon pea for drought stress tolerance.
RNA-seq investigated drought-related genes and constructed a network of faba bean drought stress response and tolerance. Transcriptome assembly generated 26,728 differentially expressed genes.
In pigeon pea (Cajanus cajan L.), we identified 28 CIPK genes. Five CcCIPK genes were upregulated in roots under drought.
SbbHLH85 is a new atypical bHLH transcription factor and a key gene for root development in sweet sorghum. It differs from its homologs in other species.

Pep1 treatment up-regulated the pathogenesis-related protein 1 (PR1) gene in leaves, activating systemic acquired resistance. Pep1 triggers transcriptional reprogramming in roots, induces oxidative stress, and activates systemic defense signaling in foxtail millet.
The SNP marker Pc94-SNP1a was tested on two F-2:3 populations segregating for the resistance gene. The map distances between the SNP marker and Pc94 were 2.1 and 5.4 cM in the two populations.
FeR2R3-MYB expression is predominant in flowering tissue and responsive to light, drought, and cold. The promoter of FeR2R3-MYB correlates positively with fragment length.

Tissue-specific expression analysis showed expression of all 11 SiCOL genes in foxtail millet tissues, especially leaves and panicles. Collinearity analysis identified 14 syntenic gene pairs in foxtail millet and rice.

During the germination period, after 7 days of simulated drought treatment, the germination rate, root length and bud length of the sinrx1 mutant decreased significantly compared to the WT. During the seedling stage under drought stress, the survival rate, chlorophyll content, proline content, peroxidase (POD) activity and catalase (CAT) activity of the sinrx1 mutant decreased markedly compared to the WT.
SiDREB genes are unevenly distributed across nine foxtail millet chromosomes. qRT-PCR experiments show that 19 SiDREB genes respond to abiotic stress and ABA.

Hulled and hulless oat cultivars had similar lodging susceptibility to nitrogen addition. They produced lower dry weight, lower kernel nitrogen, and lower grain yield than the wheat cultivar. Higher vegetative dry-matter accumulation at heading and higher phosphorus content in culms under high nitrogen supply may be related to severe lodging in oat cultivars.

Foreground selection used up to 11 polymorphic SSR markers linked to Striga resistance QTLs. Background selection in the BC3F2 generation used 27 polymorphic unlinked SSR markers to recover the recurrent parent genetic background. Among 84 BC3F3 lines, L2P3-B, L1P5-A and L2P5P35 performed best for grain yield and reduced Striga infestation.

A previous study identified QTL for early-season performance in a recombinant inbred population derived from the cold-tolerant Chinese line 'Shan Qui Red' (SQR). Three SSR markers (Xtxp43, Xtxp51, and Xtxp211), each representing a QTL, were tested in two populations: (Tx2794 x SQR F-3) and (Wheatland x SQR BC1F3).

SbbHLH85 overexpression increased root hair number and length via ABA and auxin signaling pathways, increasing Na+ absorption. SbbHLH85 negatively regulates salt tolerance in sorghum. A yeast two-hybrid screen identified phosphate transporter chaperone PHF1 as a potential interaction partner of SbbHLH85, modulating phosphate distribution.

The population showed significant genetic diversity and expressed antioxidant traits with variability and high correlation. The S. bicolor x S. halepense lines outperformed Sorghum bicolor populations for all antioxidants.

text: Three putative homoeologous groups (OM7, OM8, OM18; OM2, OM23; OM13, OM16) were identified. Nine OM linkage groups were homologous to linkage groups in the published KO map.

Association mapping was performed for plant height, panicle height, number of leaves per plant, field fresh grain weight, dry grain weight, and chlorophyll with 6,094,317 SNP markers for Striga resistance genes in diverse sorghum breeding lines and varieties. Chromosomes containing significant SNPs in FASTmrMLM and FarmCPU models were identified.
The QTL qDTH2 is on chromosome 2. The QTL qDTH7 is on chromosome 7.

Transcriptome analysis revealed key drought-responsive pathways: starch and sucrose metabolism, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, plant hormone signaling, and photosynthesis regulation. Candidate genes associated with drought response included CWINV, GPI, SPP, UGP, SS, and SBE for carbohydrate metabolism; PAL, OMT1, 4CL, and CCR for phenylpropanoid biosynthesis; and SnRK2, HAB2, GBF4, JAZ1, and MYC2 for hormone signaling.
Canggu 5 was moderately resistant to stem lodging, similar to Jigu 19. Its grain yields were consistently better than Jigu 19.

Dry was deleted or acquired loss-of-function mutations in sweet sorghum, resulting in cell collapse and altered secondary cell wall composition in the stem. Twenty-three Dry ancestral haplotypes, all with dry, pithy stems, were found among wild sorghum and wild sorghum relatives.

Inheritance of plant height was studied in Zhepi 1 and Aizao 3. Allelism tests were conducted for Zhepi 1 and Aizao 3 with barley entries carrying brh 1, uzu 1, sdw 1, or six new dwarfing genes, using direct cross and para-backcross methods.

Expression analysis of four sorghum genes encoding homologs of dormancy genes Vp1 (maize), DOG1 (Arabidopsis), qSD12 and Sdr4 (rice) was conducted. There were clear differences in expression level of all four genes between two parental lines with different seed dormancy level. Their expression was tissue-specific. The expression levels of qSD12 were low throughout all growth stages and tissues in B140.

The glutinous sorghum variety HongYingZi was treated with 200 μM cadmium acetate. Samples were collected at eight time points: control, and 3h, 6h, 9h, 12h, 24h, 48h, 72h after treatment for transcriptome sequencing. The sequencing data was filtered, aligned, assembled for transcripts, and differentially expressed genes were identified using software fastp, Hisat2, StringTie, and DEGseq.
FeDREB1, a CBF/DREB homologous gene, was isolated from Fagopyrum esculentum. Protein sequence alignment and phylogenetic analyses grouped FeDREB1 into the DREB (A-1) lineage.
56 SiDREB genes were located on nine foxtail millet chromosomes. The genes were unevenly distributed across these chromosomes.

The inbred line 'BTx623' of sorghum was analyzed for spatiotemporal transcriptome and metabolome profiles during seed development. Morphological and molecular analyses identified key seed maturation stages. Starch biosynthesis onset occurs at 5 days post-anthesis (dpa). Protein biosynthesis onset occurs at 10 dpa.
